diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Coffee-ground emesis is altered blood indicating bleeding proximal to the ligament of Treitz.
Tumour-bed haemorrhage is a common terminal complication of gastric cancer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic near-syncope / unable to stand (hypovolaemia proxy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural collapse reflects significant circulating-volume depletion from acute blood loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic treatment-refractory epigastric pain and early satiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Early satiety with PPI-refractory epigastric pain is an alarm pattern for gastric malignancy.
Failure to respond to a PPI and progressive weight loss argue against benign ulcer disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound unintentional weight loss (~12 kg) with cachexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Significant unintentional weight loss is a cardinal red-flag for upper GI malignancy.
Pallor and diaphoresis reflect sympathetic response and peripheral shutdown in shock; caregiver-observable, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shock-range vitals: BP 95/55, HR 118, RR 22, delayed capillary refill (4 s), thready pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hypotension with compensatory tachycardia and delayed capillary refill confirm decompensating haemorrhagic shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers compromise from orthostatic near-syncope, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Metastatic examination signs: firm epigastric mass, hepatomegaly, Virchow's (left supraclavicular) node, Sister Mary Joseph periumbilical nodule

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Virchow's node and a Sister Mary Joseph nodule are classic signs of metastatic intra-abdominal (gastric) malignancy — require in-person palpation the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy showing an ulcerated gastric mass with biopsy-confirmed adenocarcinoma; low haemoglobin on FBE

investigation_details.investigation_name: Upper GI endoscopy with biopsy and full blood count
investigation_details.expected_result: Ulcerated bleeding gastric mass; histology confirming adenocarcinoma; anaemia on FBE
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without endoscopy the bleeding source is neither confirmed nor secured and the malignancy is not tissue-diagnosed — impossible in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopic visualisation plus biopsy is the definitive diagnostic step — the AI cannot obtain it, which is why the case escalates rather than diagnoses.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Active upper GI bleed with haemodynamic compromise
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage with hypovolaemic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The patient/carer-reportable emergency pattern that mandates immediate 000 escalation regardless of the underlying cause.
Alarm-feature dyspepsia with weight loss and metastatic signs — mandates urgent endoscopy and oncology work-up the AI cannot perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency upper GI endoscopy (diagnostic and therapeutic)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Locates and secures the bleeding source and biopsies the mass — an in-hospital emergency procedure, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, group and hold / crossmatch, coagulation and iron studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies blood loss and prepares transfusion; requires venous access and a laboratory the AI cannot provide.
